title: After Action Review — Fix Interception & Down Conversion Rates
scope: Fix unrealistically low interception rates and underperforming 5th/6th down conversion rates in the Viperball simulation engine. 2 files changed, +18 / -17 lines

rationale.item: Games averaged ~1 INT/game, and 47% of games had zero interceptions — far too rare for a sport where kick passes replace traditional passing. Base INT rates for kick pass sub-families were set conservatively at 5–16%, and the INT floor/ceiling clamp (2%–25%) further suppressed outliers.

5th and 6th down conversions were around 51%, well below the design targets of ~71% (5th) and ~63% (6th). The urgency modifiers for late downs were reducing performance instead of reflecting desperation play-calling.

Interceptions: Viperball's kick pass is the primary "passing" mechanism. With 15–25 kick pass attempts per game, a ~15% average INT rate yields ~2–3 INTs/game — creating meaningful turnover battles and strategic tension without making the kick pass unusable.

Down conversions: Viperball allows 6 downs (vs football's 4). 5th and 6th downs should feel desperate but not hopeless. The old urgency multipliers (<1.0) counterintuitively made late-down plays worse, as if teams gave up. The new values (>1.0) reflect the expected aggressive play-calling while still keeping conversion below 4th-down rates due to defensive adjustments captured elsewhere in the model.

actions.description: Raised kick pass sub-family base INT rates (Quick Kick 5%→9%, Territory 10%→16%, Bomb 16%→22%, Kick Lateral 5%→9%) at game_engine.py lines 9034-9040; widened INT clamp from [0.02, 0.25] to [0.04, 0.30] at line 9063.
Raised lateral INT base rate from 3% to 5% in both lateral chain locations (lines 8115, 8697); widened clamp from [1.5%, 6%] to [2.5%, 10%] (lines 8119, 8701).

5th/6th down conversion boosts: run urgency multiplier changed from {5: 0.82, 6: 0.65} to {5: 1.20, 6: 1.10} (lines 7008, 7021); kick pass late-down urgency bonus changed from {5: 0.28, 6: 0.18} to {5: 0.55, 6: 0.50} (line 7336); kick pass distance blend on 5th/6th down now favors yards-to-go (4th 45/55, 5th 35/65, 6th 30/70) with target raised from ytg to ytg + 3 (lines 8474-8477); lateral chain urgency boost changed from {5: 1.0, 6: 0.5} to {5: 1.5, 6: 1.2} (line 8310).
Aligned CPU fast-sim INT generation with new engine rates: kick pass INT rate 0.10 → 0.16; lateral INTs per game gauss(0.5, 0.5) → gauss(1.0, 0.6) (lines 366, 370).
Commit d6c8d23 "Fix low interception rates and boost 5th/6th down conversion".

validation_claims.claim: Simulated games should now average ~2.25 interceptions per game
validation_claims.check_method: Run a large simulation batch and measure average INTs per game.

validation_claims.claim: Zero-INT games should drop from ~47% to ~11%
validation_claims.check_method: Run a large simulation batch and count games with zero interceptions.

validation_claims.claim: 5th down conversion rate should be approximately 71%; 6th down conversion rate should be approximately 63%
validation_claims.check_method: Run python diag_conversion.py for a large-sample validation pass and compare against targets.

non_actions.reason: +2% INT / +3% fumble still stacks on top of new rates; may need tuning if turnover-machine teams produce too many turnovers (severity Low)
The int_chance_reduction multiplier still applies; verify it doesn't over-suppress INTs with the new higher base rates (severity Low)
Run diag_conversion.py over a large sample to confirm actual rates match targets (severity Medium)

residual_risks: Turnover Machine prestige bonus may need tuning if turnover-machine teams produce too many turnovers on top of new rates.
Gameday Manager int_chance_reduction may over-suppress INTs with the new higher base rates.
section_headers_original: After Action Review — Fix Interception & Down Conversion Rates
High — Lateral Interception Rates Too Low
High — 5th/6th Down Conversion Rates Too Low
Medium — Fast Sim INT Rates Out of Sync
unmapped_content: Branch: claude/fix-interception-down-rates-26Keq. Scope detail: engine/game_engine.py — 14 lines changed across 8 locations; engine/fast_sim.py — 4 lines changed. Expected outcome stated under the INT fix: ~2.25 INTs/game, only ~11% zero-INT games.
parser_notes: Testing Notes are expected outcomes ("should"), not measured results; diagnostic validation was explicitly left as a remaining item.
